# Generated single-signal FT-8 fixtures (M2 acquisition slice)

**Deterministic known-answer WAVs** for the M2 real-WAV thin end-to-end KAT
(plan `docs/plans/2026-07-05-station-intel-l0-ft8-decoder-plan.md`, task T2.3).
Distinct from `../sdr/` — those are real off-air RTL-SDR captures for the M3/M4
oracle-parity gate; these are noiseless, single-signal, encoder-generated frames
whose exact transmitted message is known by construction (no oracle needed).

## Provenance (clean-room)

Generated by **MIT `ft8_lib` (kgoba) `gen_ft8`**, commit `9fec6ca`, the
permissive independent encoder — NOT by this crate's own code and NOT by the GPL
WSJT-X. Using an independent encoder makes the T2.3 KAT a true cross-implementation
check of the decoder, not a self-consistency round-trip.

Regenerate (ft8_lib is MIT; re-clone if the scratch copy is gone):

```
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/kgoba/ft8_lib && cd ft8_lib && make gen_ft8
./gen_ft8 "<MESSAGE>" <out.wav> <FREQ_HZ>
```

## Format (all files)

- 12000 Hz, mono, 16-bit PCM, 15.000 s (180000 samples). Canonical FT-8 decode rate.
- **GFSK-shaped** 8-FSK (Gaussian BT smoothing) — exactly what WSJT-X transmits;
  the noncoherent per-tone-power demod reads the symbol-center energy regardless.
- Tone spacing 6.25 Hz; `FREQ_HZ` is the tone-0 (lowest) audio frequency.

## Signal placement (critical for the DT sync search)

`gen_ft8` **centers** the 12.64 s (79-symbol) frame inside the 15 s slot:

- Silence pad each end = `(15·12000 − 151680)/2` = **14160 samples = 1.180 s**.
- The frame occupies sample offsets **[14160, 165840)**, i.e. **1.180 s … 13.820 s**.
- Symbol *k* (0-indexed, k=0 is the first Costas tone) starts at sample
  `14160 + k·1920` (1920 samples/symbol = 0.160 s).

The T2.2 coarse DT search must cover ~1.18 s from WAV start. (WSJT-X's own DT=0
convention places the ideal signal ~0.5 s into the slot, so gen_ft8's centered
placement is ≈ DT +0.68 s in WSJT-X terms — but the decoder searches the raw
sample offset above; do not hard-code a WSJT-X DT offset.)

## Known-answer manifest

| File | Exact message (assert this) | Msg type | Tone-0 freq |
|------|-----------------------------|----------|-------------|
| `std_cq_1500.wav`    | `CQ K1ABC FN42` | 1 standard         | 1500 Hz |
| `freetext_1500.wav`  | `TNX BOB 73 GL` | 0.0 free-text      | 1500 Hz |
| `nonstd_cq_1500.wav` | `CQ PJ4/K1ABC`  | 4 nonstandard call | 1500 Hz |
| `std_cq_0800.wav`    | `CQ K1ABC FN42` | 1 standard         |  800 Hz |
| `std_cq_2400.wav`    | `CQ K1ABC FN42` | 1 standard         | 2400 Hz |
| `std_cq_1509_offgrid.wav` | `CQ K1ABC FN42` | 1 standard    | 1509 Hz |

`std_cq_1509_offgrid.wav` is a carrier deliberately **off** the 3.125 Hz coarse
search grid: the dB-contrast coarse metric lands its top candidate >1 tone away
(≈1515.6 Hz), so it exercises the fine-refine frequency window's ability to
recover a true carrier the coarse stage mislocates by more than one tone.

The three frequencies for the standard message exercise the T2.2 coarse frequency
search across the audio passband (do not overfit the search to 1500 Hz). Types 1,
0.0, and 4 are the three the `9fec6ca` text encoder packs cleanly; types 3
(RTTY-RU) and 0.5 (telemetry) return RC=5 from its `ftx_message_encode` and are
already covered by the M0 pack/unpack KATs, not needed for the acquisition proof.

Message strings above are the ground truth: T2.3 asserts `unpack(decode(wav)) == message`.
